prompt: Develop a LensWork Alumni Photography Marketplace system for alumni communities to engage verified photographers for event and portrait jobs while maintaining strict privacy and compliance controls. The React-based web interface provides three roles: Alumni User, Photographer, and Administrator. Users create local accounts with username and password, sign in, and manage a profile where each sensitive field (phone, email, employer) can be set to Public, Alumni-Only, or Private; when Private, the UI shows masked values (for example, (555) ***-1234) and supports authorized access requests that the user can approve or deny within 7 days. Photographers maintain a portfolio with optional visible watermarks on previews and copyright notices on each deliverable, and can accept work only after completing a real-name and qualification submission workflow. For each job, both sides review a service agreement on-screen and e-confirm by typing their account password; the system then allows timesheet entry (15-minute increments) or piece-rate line items, with a two-step bilateral confirmation that locks entries after 48 hours. A settlement page generates a statement with any adjustments and a variance reason required if the final amount differs by more than 2% or $25.00 (whichever is greater); because the system must run fully offline, collections/refunds are recorded as “offline payment” with receipt details and optional escrow deposit tracking as a local ledger entry rather than a network payment. Users can attach invoice/receipt files (PDF/JPG/PNG up to 10 MB each) and export settlement statements to a local file for bookkeeping. Content safety is handled through built-in sensitive-word checks, manual review status (pending/approved/rejected) for portfolios and job messages, and a report center where users submit category, written description, and file evidence with visible progress states.The backend uses Express to expose decoupled REST-style APIs consumed by the React client over HTTPS on the local network or a single machine, with all data persisted in MongoDB for offline durability. Security and privacy compliance are enforced through data minimization and purpose disclosure at first use of each data category, with consent records stored per policy version and a readable privacy policy history available in-app; users must re-consent within 30 days when a new version introduces a new purpose. Sensitive fields (government ID numbers, tax forms, qualification documents) are stored encrypted at rest using AES-256, while all sessions use signed tokens with a 15-minute idle timeout and 24-hour absolute expiration. Each API request includes a nonce and timestamp; the server rejects replays within a 10-minute window and logs all security-relevant operations (login attempts, permission changes, KYC reviews, exports, deletions) to an append-only audit log retained for 7 years. Role permissions and fine-grained resource access control ensure that only job participants and authorized admins can view or export documents; exports require an explicit permission check and are blocked for blacklisted/banned accounts. Real-name verification is implemented as a local workflow: users upload ID and qualification files that pass format and checksum validation, then an admin reviewer transitions status among submitted, needs-changes, verified, or rejected with a mandatory reason, while the system always masks sensitive identifiers in any non-admin view. Risk controls include rate limits (for example, 60 requests/minute per account and 10 reports/day), blacklist enforcement on usernames/devices via locally stored hashed device fingerprints (optional and consent-gated), and reserved internal hooks for future compliance model integration without requiring any external network dependency.
